---
title: "Parallel Computing Prac"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Questions

```{r}
library(foreach)
library(doParallel)
```

### Question 1

```{r}
calc <- function(n) {
  vals <- rexp(n, 1)
  m <- mean(vals)
  v <- var(vals)
  c(m, v)
}

c1 <- makeCluster(3)
registerDoParallel(c1)

m_v <- foreach(i = 1:100, .combine = rbind) %dopar% {
  calc(n = 1000)
}

stopCluster(c1)
```

### Question 2

```{r}
library(MASS)
gal <- galaxies

B <- 1000
n <- length(gal)

# generate bootstrap medians
m <- function(n, gal) {
  sam <- sample(gal, n, replace = T)
  med_g <- median(sam)
}

c2 <- makeCluster(3)
registerDoParallel(c2)

system.time({
b_m <- foreach(i = 1:B, .combine = rbind) %dopar% {
  m(n, gal)
}
})

stopCluster(c2)

tboot <- numeric(B)

system.time({
  for (i in 1:B) {
  tboot[i] <- median(sample(gal, length(gal), replace = TRUE))
}
})
```

### Question 3

```{r}
library(foreach)
library(doParallel)

set.seed(1)

R <- 1000
B <- 1000

cl <- makeCluster(3)
registerDoParallel(cl)

hits <- foreach(r = 1:R, .combine = "+") %dopar% {
  x <- rexp(50, rate = 1)

  boot_means <- replicate(B, mean(sample(x, 50, replace = TRUE)))
  CI <- quantile(boot_means, c(0.025, 0.975), names = FALSE)

  as.integer(CI[1] <= 1 && 1 <= CI[2])
}

stopCluster(cl)

coverage <- hits / R
coverage
```

### Question 4

```{r}
library(foreach)
library(iterators)

set.seed(1234)

it <- irnorm(5)   # infinite iterator of length-5 vectors

max_vals <- foreach(i = icount(3), .combine = c) %do% {
  v <- nextElem(it)
  max(v)
}

print(max_vals)
```

### Question 5

```{r}
# Packages
library(iterators)
library(foreach)
library(doParallel)
library(parallel)

set.seed(1234)

# --- one "run" of the task, done 3 times (3 vectors of length 5) ---

run_replicate <- function() {
  # serial base R
  replicate(3, max(rnorm(5)))
}

run_foreach <- function() {
  # uses irnorm(5) (infinite iterator) + icount(3) to stop after 3 vectors
  it <- irnorm(5)
  foreach(i = icount(3), .combine = c) %do% {
    max(nextElem(it))
  }
}

run_parLapply <- function(cl) {
  # parallel: each worker computes one max(rnorm(5))
  unlist(parLapply(cl, 1:3, function(i) max(rnorm(5))))
}

run_foreach_par <- function() {
  # parallel foreach: each worker computes one max(rnorm(5))
  foreach(i = 1:3, .combine = c) %dopar% max(rnorm(5))
}

# --- timing: repeat each method many times ---
R <- 5000  # increase if you want more stable timings

# 1) replicate (serial)
t_rep <- system.time({
  for (r in 1:R) run_replicate()
})

# 2) foreach (serial %do%) -- still useful as a baseline for foreach overhead
t_for_do <- system.time({
  for (r in 1:R) run_foreach()
})

# Set up cluster ONCE (important)
cl <- makeCluster(3)
registerDoParallel(cl)

# 3) parLapply (parallel)
t_parL <- system.time({
  for (r in 1:R) run_parLapply(cl)
})

# 4) foreach (parallel %dopar%)
t_for_par <- system.time({
  for (r in 1:R) run_foreach_par()
})

stopCluster(cl)

# Compare
times <- rbind(
  replicate = t_rep[1:3],
  foreach_do = t_for_do[1:3],
  parLapply = t_parL[1:3],
  foreach_dopar = t_for_par[1:3]
)
times
```
